feature: F2 — Auth (User model, register/login, JWT middleware)

agent_log.output_summary: Locked F2 implementation skeletons: Sequelize singleton (db/sequelize.ts), umzug-direct migration runner (db/migrate.ts), AppError hierarchy + Express error handler with full dispatch table, env.ts extended (JWT_SECRET, JWT_EXPIRES_IN, CORS_ORIGINS CSV->string[], DATABASE_URL_TEST), .sequelizerc as path-hint only, packages/shared populated with auth DTO types. Two key deviations from spec: ADR-008 picks umzug-direct over sequelize-cli (avoids TS+CJS interop fragility) and ADR-009 keeps zod schemas in apps/api with pure types in @app/shared (avoids dragging zod into the web bundle).

Implemented F2 auth: migration 0001-create-users.ts, User model with defaultScope hiding password_hash, zod auth schemas (register/login), service layer (hashPassword/verifyPassword/createUser/findByEmailLower/signToken), controller with constant-time-ish login (SENTINEL_HASH decoy + single INVALID_CREDENTIALS path), /auth router with in-file asyncHandler helper, requireAuth middleware (defensive typeof checks on JWT claims), express.d.ts type augmentation for req.user. Edited app.ts (env-driven CORS allowlist, mounted /auth, errorHandler last), index.ts (await pingDatabase fail-fast at boot), routes/health.ts (DB ping -> 503 on down), .env.example (added DATABASE_URL_TEST/JWT_EXPIRES_IN/CORS_ORIGINS, bumped JWT_SECRET placeholder). Authored ADR-010 (bcrypt cost 10) and ADR-011 (HS256/24h/{sub,email,iat,exp} payload, no refresh tokens). Fixed pre-existing TS error in architect's migrate.ts (Umzug<typeof sequelize> -> Umzug<QueryInterface>). yarn install run; tsc -p tsconfig.json clean; yarn lint clean (only architect-authored sequelize.ts no-console warning remains).

Wrote F2 integration tests against real Postgres test DB. Created tests/helpers/{setup-env,setup-global,teardown-global,server}.ts and apps/api/.env.test (gitignored). Added auth.register.test.ts (10 cases: 2 happy, 1 dup-CITEXT, 7 validation via it.each), auth.login.test.ts (5 cases: happy with JWT decode + payload assertion, case-mismatch, wrong-pwd, unknown-email/enumeration defense, malformed payload), auth.middleware.test.ts (8 cases: missing/malformed/empty header, sig mismatch, malformed JWT, expired, wrong-typed claim, valid bearer happy path with req.user assertion). Updated tests/health.test.ts to F2 contract. Wired jest globalSetup (one-time umzug runMigrations + truncate), globalTeardown (close pool), per-worker setupFiles (dotenv .env.test before env.ts loads). Switched test script to --runInBand to avoid truncate races. Bootstrapped local Postgres (campaign role + campaign_test DB) because docker daemon was hung. 24/24 tests pass; tsc clean; lint clean (only pre-existing architect no-console warning). NO source-code edits — all tests pass against backend's implementation as authored.

F2 security review. Verdict: APPROVED-WITH-NITS. Walked the full security checklist (password handling, JWT, login enumeration, email handling, error response surface, CORS, SQL injection, secrets, tests, DoD). Two BLOCKER-grade hardening fixes applied inline: (1) middleware.ts jwt.verify now pins algorithms:['HS256'] (defense-in-depth against algorithm confusion; jsonwebtoken@9 is already strict but explicit pinning matches OWASP guidance and survives any future major-version regressions); (2) app.ts express.json now sets explicit limit:'100kb' (DOS-surface contract pinned, was relying on body-parser default). All 24/24 tests still pass post-fix; tsc + lint clean. PRAISE: SENTINEL_HASH constant-time-ish login defense + identical INVALID_CREDENTIALS code; defaultScope hides password_hash at SQL level; password.max(72) explicitly handles bcrypt's 72-byte truncation foot-gun; JWT_SECRET length policy enforced via .superRefine; defensive typeof check on JWT claims; helmet first in middleware chain; .env.test gitignored AND not tracked. MEDIUM tech-debt logged: login rate-limit (out of F2 scope), JWT_EXPIRES_IN regex validation (silent-instant-expiry footgun), Zod err.issues echoed in details (Zod-major upgrade re-audit). Workflow status flipped to 'verified'. Devops next.

Wired GitHub Actions CI to run F2's DB-backed test suite. Updated .github/workflows/ci.yml: added postgres:16-alpine service container (matches docker-compose.yml; healthcheck pg_isready interval 5s/timeout 3s/retries 10), pinned host port 5432:5432 so DATABASE_URL_TEST resolves; added pre-test step that creates campaign_test via `docker run --rm postgres:16-alpine psql -c 'CREATE DATABASE campaign_test OWNER campaign'` against the service container's admin DB (the postgres image only auto-creates ONE DB; tests need a dedicated one); added pre-test step that writes apps/api/.env.test inline via heredoc (the local file is gitignored so CI can't import it; the test infra dotenv-loads from that fixed path — option (a) per the task brief); test-only env vars (DATABASE_URL, DATABASE_URL_TEST, JWT_SECRET 40-char ASCII, JWT_EXPIRES_IN=1h, CORS_ORIGINS, NODE_ENV=test, PORT=4001) are plain workflow env (NOT GitHub secrets) — documented inline that they only authenticate the ephemeral service container. Lint runs BEFORE test for fast feedback. Kept --frozen-lockfile. README updated with two test paths (Option A docker compose; Option B host Postgres) and three troubleshooting cases (role campaign missing, campaign_test DB missing, env loader exit on missing DATABASE_URL_TEST); stack-table CI row updated. Re-ran yarn workspace @app/api test locally post-edit: 24/24 passing in ~1.9s. Did NOT add Redis (F3), did NOT introduce test-stage Dockerfile, did NOT modify .env.test or any test source.
